description_long: This cabin consists of two parallel rectangular structures joined by a central raised walkway, with a set of stairs leading up to the entrance. The roofs are steeply pitched and covered in weathered, grayish wooden shingles, giving the building a rustic appearance. The exterior walls are a mix of corrugated metal panels and wooden planks, with the metal showing signs of rust and age. The cabin is elevated on stilts, suggesting it is designed for areas prone to flooding or uneven terrain. The front features open porches under the roof eaves, while the sides and rear are more enclosed. The overall design is utilitarian, with minimal ornamentation and a focus on practicality.
description: A rustic, stilted twin-cabin structure with steep gable roofs, weathered wood, and corrugated metal siding, connected by a central walkway and front porches.
description_view_0: Front view showing the twin cabins, central staircase, and walkway leading to open porches beneath the gable roofs.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the long, continuous gable roof, corrugated metal siding, and the stilted elevation of the structure.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the twin cabins, with visible metal and wood paneling and the walkway between the two units.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the extended roof, stilt supports, and a mix of metal and wood siding.
attribute_roof_type: steep gable roof with extended overhangs, covered in weathered wooden shingles
attribute_window_arrangement: Few small rectangular windows, asymmetrically placed on the sides and front, with some grouped under the porch eaves
attribute_door_placement: Two main doors centered on the front facade of each cabin, accessible via the central walkway and stairs
